chapter_title: 9. Normal administrative practice – records that do not need to be archived
snippet: Some information can be routinely destroyed following a process known as Normal Administrative Practice. Normal Administrative Practice allows the routine disposal of information that is not an official school record, examples include: Refer to Chapter 10 – How to destroy ‘time-expired’ records for guidance on how to formally dispose of official school records. 8. Includes steps on how to destroy ‘time-expired’ records Includes information on records that can be routinely destroyed following a process known as Normal Administrative Practice.
